verdicts.rationale: Line 1: REPORT zabapgit_standalone LINE-SIZE 100. Lines 7261-7266: INTERFACE zif_abapgit_version with CONSTANTS c_xml_version = 'v1.0.0' and c_abap_version = '1.133.0' - both re-read directly from the source and match the sentence exactly.

Lines 152971-152972: START-OF-SELECTION. PERFORM run. Lines 152802-152820 (re-read, FORM run): TRY block checks zcl_abapgit_auth=>is_allowed( ...startup ) = abap_false -> raise; then zcl_abapgit_migrations=>run( ); PERFORM open_gui; CATCH clauses. Matches the claimed sequence exactly.

Re-read lines 152822-152848 (FORM open_gui): IF sy-batch = abap_true -> zcl_abapgit_background=>run( ); ELSE branch does lcl_startup=>prepare_gui_startup( ), zcl_abapgit_ui_factory=>get_gui( )->go_home( lv_action ), CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001. Exact match.
Lines 152827-152828 (re-read): IF sy-batch = abap_true. zcl_abapgit_background=>run( ). Confirms the batch-mode delegation exactly as stated.

Re-read lines 152376-152409 in full (incl. the '4 lines omitted' block): SCREEN 1001 empty/dummy (comment 'dummy for triggering screen'); SCREEN 1002 TITLE sc_title contains PARAMETERS p_url (152393), p_user (152398), p_pass (152401-152402, in the omitted range), p_cmnt (152407). All 4 parameters and both screens confirmed.

Lines 152559-152573 (re-read): FORM password_popup calls lcl_password_dialog=>popup CHANGING cv_user/cv_pass. Lines 152465-152474 (re-read, METHOD popup): after CALL SELECTION-SCREEN c_dynnr, IF gv_confirm = abap_true THEN cv_user = p_user / cv_pass = p_pass ELSE CLEAR cv_user, cv_pass. Exact match, correct branch direction.

Re-read full FORM adjust_toolbar (152904-152965): CALL FUNCTION 'RPY_DYNPRO_READ' at 152912, CALL FUNCTION 'RPY_DYNPRO_INSERT' at 152942, with lv_no_toolbar computed from is_variant_maintenance( ) in between (152931-152940) - confirms the toggle for variant maintenance.

Re-read FORM output (152850-152876): APPEND 'CRET'/'SPOS' TO lt_ucomm (Execute/Save), then CALL FUNCTION 'RS_SET_SELSCREEN_STATUS' EXPORTING p_status = sy-pfkey TABLES p_exclude = lt_ucomm. Exact match.

All 4 re-read and confirmed verbatim: line 84505 SELECT SINGLE author FROM tadir (author lookup); line 84503 SELECT SINGLE as4user FROM e070; line 72474 FROM e071 (SELECT DISTINCT pgmid object obj_name, lines 72472-72476); line 74899 SELECT SINGLE progname FROM reposrc; line 139920 SELECT SINGLE subc ... FROM trdir. All four tables genuinely read as claimed.

Line 95845 SUBMIT scpr3 AND RETURN, inside METHOD zif_abapgit_object~jump of CLASS zcl_abapgit_object_scp1 (class starts 95511; EXPORT scpr3_bcset_id at 95839-95843 confirms BC-Set handling). The same class implements zif_abapgit_object~serialize (95856) and ~deserialize (95774) a few lines away, confirming it genuinely is an abapGit 'serializer' (object handler) class, not a mislabeled one.
Line 152855 (re-read, inside FORM output): PERFORM set_pf_status IN PROGRAM rsdbrunt IF FOUND. Exact match, external PERFORM into standard program rsdbrunt confirmed.
wc -l on the actual source file returns 152996 lines exactly, matching the claimed physical line count.

grep '^FORM \w+' over the whole file returns exactly 6 matches: password_popup (152559), run (152802), open_gui (152822), output (152850), exit (152878), adjust_toolbar (152904). Names and count match exactly.

Whole-file scan for SUBMIT found many case-insensitive hits, but almost all are JS/HTML string literals inside lo_buf->add(...)/ri_html->add(...) generator code. Filtering to real ABAP SUBMIT statements yields exactly 5, each verified in context: line 40617 SUBMIT (sy-cprog); 95845 SUBMIT scpr3 AND RETURN; 100579 SUBMIT (lv_prog) ... AND RETURN; 100918 SUBMIT (lv_prog) ... AND RETURN; 119357 SUBMIT (lv_report) WITH SELECTION-TABLE lt_params AND RETURN.
grep 'SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF SCREEN' over the whole file returns exactly 2 matches: line 152376 (SCREEN 1001) and line 152389 (SCREEN 1002). Matches the claim exactly.

Total count of 'INTERFACE ... DEFERRED' in the file is exactly 113 (verified by grep), so the number is correct. However the phrase 'opens with 113' overstates the structure: only 107 of them form the contiguous opening block (lines 29-135, right after the license header); the remaining 6 (iUFTs... random-named interfaces at lines 17346, 17450, 43162, 63421, 136670, 136676) are scattered deep in the file, not part of the opening. The count is right but the 'opens with' framing is not.

Lines 152992-152995 and line 1 confirm the file is a single 152996-line report bearing an abapmerge 0.16.8 marker with timestamp 2026-07-01T10:45:14.302Z - those facts are proven by code (CONSTANTS, not comments). The prescriptive conclusion 'must be replaced wholesale ... never hand-edited' is a reasonable maintenance inference from the generated-file marker but is not itself asserted anywhere in the cited lines; it is not a defect literally shown by the code.

The three cited snippets independently confirm: the startup auth gate (152808-152810, IF is_allowed(startup) = abap_false -> raise), the RPY_DYNPRO_INSERT call (152942), and the SUBMIT scpr3 AND RETURN call (95845). No local authorization check is visible around either of the two powerful calls (adjust_toolbar and the scp1 jump method), which is consistent with the claim. However the qualifier 'gated ... only by' is a global negative that cannot be proven from these 3 isolated snippets alone - there could be additional checks elsewhere in the 152996-line file not covered by the citation.

The 'bundles the ajson library (v1.1.13)' and 'merged by abapmerge 0.16.8' parts are proven by real code: CONSTANTS version = 'v1.1.13' (line 2058) and CONSTANTS c_abapmerge_version = '0.16.8' (line 152995). The 'MIT-licensed' part of the sentence, however, is evidenced only by line 6 '* The MIT License (MIT)', which is a comment (column-1 '*') - per the comments-are-not-evidence rule this portion is not validly evidenced, even though it happens to be true.

The cited lines (47419-47420) only show two HTML table rows displaying the abapGit/XML version numbers on an about-page; they do not show or prove 'Git-based version control and object transport for ABAP objects'. That functional characterization is standard domain knowledge about what abapGit is, not something demonstrated by the cited lines. The self-hedge about in-house usage being organisational/not provable is accurate, but the core functional assertion is not anchored to the cited evidence.

The file is genuinely 152996 lines (verified). Independent counts confirm the scale claim: 390 occurrences of standalone SELECT (claim says '~387', within ~1%) and 626 'CLASS ... DEFINITION' occurrences (including deferred and real, confirming 'hundreds of internal classes'). This self-referential statement about L1's inability to profile the full runtime call tree is a reasonable, well-supported acknowledgment of scale rather than a specific code assertion.

Case-insensitive whole-file search for 'ltcl_' returns exactly one hit (line 145675), and it is a string literal '\PROGRAM={ sy-repid }\CLASS=LTCL_TEST' used inside cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name to detect (via RTTI) whether a test class exists at runtime - not an actual 'CLASS ltcl_... DEFINITION' local test class. No local test class definition exists anywhere in the file, confirming the claim.

Lines 152469-152470 (re-read): IF gv_confirm = abap_true. cv_user = p_user. - confirms P_USER is copied to cv_user only on confirm. cv_user is confirmed (CL-006) to be the CHANGING parameter of FORM password_popup, which is returned to its caller.

Lines 152469-152471 (re-read) confirm the primary mapping: IF gv_confirm = abap_true. cv_user = p_user. cv_pass = p_pass. - P_PASS copied to cv_pass only on confirm. However the additional assertion 'field rendered invisible in on_screen_output' is true (verified at lines 152506-152507: IF screen-name = 'P_PASS'. screen-invisible = '1'.) but that location is NOT part of the cited evidence range (152469-152471) - an out-of-range citation for that part of the sentence.

Lines 152495-152500 (re-read, METHOD on_screen_output) confirm the primary target: IF screen-name = 'P_URL' OR screen-name = 'P_CMNT'. screen-input = '0' ... MODIFY SCREEN. - P_URL disabled as claimed. However 'Set from iv_repo_url before the dialog' is true (verified at line 152453: p_url = iv_repo_url.) but that line is outside the cited range (152495-152500) - out-of-range for that clause.

Lines 152843-152844 (re-read) show zcl_abapgit_ui_factory=>get_gui( )->go_home( lv_action ). CALL SELECTION-SCREEN 1001. This is the entry point that triggers the HTML rendering chain; the sentence itself acknowledges the actual HTML production happens inside the GUI framework (not shown at this exact line), consistent with the 'computed' kind definition (non-DDIC, no sy-* value).
Lines 152814-152815 (re-read, inside FORM run): CATCH zcx_abapgit_exception INTO lx_exception. MESSAGE lx_exception TYPE 'E'. Exact match - message text computed from the caught exception object.
Lines 152816-152817 (re-read, inside FORM run): CATCH zcx_abapgit_not_found INTO lx_not_found. MESSAGE lx_not_found TYPE 'E'. Exact match.
Lines 152871-152872 (re-read, inside FORM output): CATCH zcx_abapgit_exception INTO lx_error. MESSAGE lx_error TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'. Exact match.
